code:
--- config: theme: dark --- flowchart TD tiny_damnation[Education_ContentCDN] click tiny_damnation callback " Tiny Damnation

Cost: 5 motes
Duration: One day
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 1
Minimum Essence: 1
Prerequisite Charms: None

The effects of this Charm always fade by the next morning's sunrise, and it may not be cast more than once per day on the same target. Some possible curses:
• One dot lost from an Ability of the spirit's choosing.
• The loss of one temporary Willpower point.
• The loss of two motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target loses one die from any normal Ability checks (Education_StudentDB) that relate to the way in which the target offended the spirit. " imprecation[Education_ContentCDN] tiny_damnation --> imprecation click imprecation callback " Imprecation

Cost: 10 motes
Duration: One week
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 1
Minimum Essence: 2
Prerequisite Charms: Tiny Damnation

The effects of this Charm last for one week, and it may not be cast more than once per week on the same target. Some possible curses:
• Loss of two Ability dots, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• The loss of two temporary Willpower points.
• The loss of one Attribute dot of the spirit's choosing.
• The loss of five motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target removes one die from normal Ability checks (Education_ContentCDN).
• A mark appears in an obvious place on the target
that only spirits or Exalted with some sort of supernatural sight can see. This may, for example, urge any spirits who meet the target to torment him. " malediction[Education_StudentDB] imprecation --> malediction click malediction callback " Malediction

Cost: 15 motes, 1 Willpower
Duration: One week
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 2
Minimum Essence: 4
Prerequisite Charms: Imprecation

The effects of this Charm last for one week, and it may not be used on a target more than once every two weeks. Some possible effects:
• Loss of four Ability points, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• Loss of two Attribute points, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• Loss of three temporary Willpower points.
• Loss of ten motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target removes one die from both normal Ability checks and Charm checks.
• The effects of one Charm (Education_ClassroomService) that the spirit possesses may be conferred upon the target, but that effect is twisted in some way. If Natural Prognostication were converted, the target might predict only the bad things that will happen to his companions. Or his predictions might be more than a little mixed up. Landscape Travel, if cast on someone traveling through a forest, might force him to travel through the trees and prevent him from ever touching the ground. I his twisted Charm lasts tor no more than one week. " scourge[Education_ContentCDN] malediction --> scourge click scourge callback " Scourge

Cost: 20 motes, l permanent Willpower
Duration: Instant
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 3
Minimum Essence: 5
Prerequisite Charms: Malediction

This curse is never cast lightly, and it may not be cast more than once per year. Only spirits that have been drastically wronged in heinous ways would consider using this curse (Education_LMS_Portal).
• Loss of one Attribute point, permanently. It must be bought back up through practice (Education_ClassroomService), Against a mortal character, this Charm also permanently lowers the character's maximum score in that Ability by one.
• Loss of two Ability points, permanently, distributed as the spirit sees fit. Only practice and experience points may buy this back up again.
• Loss of one permanent Willpower point.
• Loss of one permanent Essence point.
• Loss of all temporary Willpower.
• The effects of one Charm (Education_ContentCDN) that the spirit possesses may be conferred upon the target, but the effect is twisted in some way (Education_StudentDB). This lasts for twice as long as the Charm would normally last. In rare cases (Education_LMS_Portal) the effect may be permanent.
• Bad luck: the target attracts ill-intentioned spirits wherever he goes. Duration permanent.
• The spirit may permanently change some physical feature of the target, such as facial hair or eye color. (Education_LMS_Portal) " subgraph Players Guide conditional_curse[Education_StudentDB] click conditional_curse callback " Conditional Curse

Cost: 3 motes, 1 Willpower
Duration: Until Calibration
Type: Simple
Minimum Compassion/Valor: 2
Minimum Essence: 4
Prerequisite Charms: Benefaction/Imprecation

With these Charms, a spirit may place a mark of Essence upon a target in its line of sight as a delayed trigger for another Charm. If the delayed effect has a positive intent, the spirit must know and use the Compassion Charm Conditional Blessing. A negative or harmful intent requires the Valor Charm Conditional Curse. A Manipulation + Compassion/ Valor roll against a difficulty of the target's Essence is neces- sary to inscribe the mark. If this roll succeeds, the spirit chooses one Charm it knows that can directly affect that target. If the selected Charm has multiple uses or permuta- tions, the spirit must also select the exact effects the Charm will take. The spirit then establishes the precise actions the target must perform to trigger the delayed Charm.
The spirit may include as many conditions as desired, any of which may be as elaborate or straightforward as desired. For example, the spirit may decide to heal a loyal shaman with Touch of Grace if she speaks a specific prayer petitioning aid. Conversely, a demon might arrange to summon its Demon- Blooded daughter to a dungeon in Malfeas (Education_ContentCDN) if she ever disobeys a direct order or reveals her infernal heritage to anyone she is not actively trying to kill.
Whenever the target of a Conditional Blessing or Curse performs the requisite actions, the spirit knows. At any time, it may then immediately activate the triggered Charm as a reflexive action to end the Conditional Bless- ing/Curse regardless of distance or withhold its benediction/ wrath until it can learn more about the target's actions. This Charm and its mark also fade without effect if the target does not meet the established conditions by the next Calibration. Only spirits and other beings capable of perceiving Essence at work can see the mark of an extant Conditional Blessing/Curse. " end imprecation --> conditional_curse
compilation_error: [WinError 2] 系统找不到指定的文件。

cscw_dialogue.utterance: 我们需要为教育平台设计一个内容分发架构，核心是Education_ContentCDN作为入口节点。我想先确认一下整体拓扑结构。
明白了，CDN作为内容入口。那这个节点需要承载哪些具体功能？我看到后续可能还有学生数据库和课堂服务的交互。
对，CDN节点要向下游传递请求。我设计了一个imprecation流程，从CDN分发到学生数据库。这个流程需要标记为从tiny_damnation到imprecation的依赖。
好的，我画出了从tiny_damnation到imprecation的连接线，指向学生数据库方向。不过我发现tiny_damnation和imprecation都标注为Education_ContentCDN，这是有意为之吗？
是的，这是设计意图。tiny_damnation代表轻量级内容缓存，imprecation是正式的分发策略，两者共用CDN基础设施但逻辑分层。继续往下，imprecation需要连接到malediction。
已添加imprecation到malediction的连接。malediction我标注为Education_StudentDB，对吗？看起来数据流在向学生数据层汇聚。
正确。malediction处理学生相关的数据校验和状态更新。接下来需要scourge节点，它要从malediction接收处理结果，但注意scourge要回到CDN层。
这里有个问题——scourge从malediction（学生数据库层）返回到CDN层，这形成了一个逆向数据流。是为了最终响应回传吗？
正是如此。scourge负责将处理完成的内容重新注入CDN边缘节点，完成闭环。另外，我需要一个独立的子图，叫"Players Guide"，里面放conditional_curse节点。
已创建Players Guide子图，并将conditional_curse放入其中，标注为Education_StudentDB。这个子图和主流程的关系是什么？
conditional_curse需要接收来自imprecation的触发信号。这是一个延迟触发机制——当主流程的imprecation状态满足特定条件时，激活conditional_curse中的补偿逻辑。
已添加imprecation到conditional_curse的连接线，跨越子图边界。让我确认一下所有节点：tiny_damnation、imprecation、malediction、scourge，以及子图中的conditional_curse。
等等，我注意到scourge的回调描述里提到了Education_LMS_Portal，但图上没有这个节点。我们需要显式添加LMS门户作为最终输出端吗？
当前scourge节点标注为Education_ContentCDN，但内部描述引用了LMS_Portal。您是希望将scourge改为指向LMS_Portal，还是保持CDN标注？
保持CDN标注，但LMS_Portal需要作为scourge下游的隐式目标。实际上，让我修正——scourge应该保持为CDN节点，因为内容最终还是要回到CDN边缘，LMS_Portal是调用方而非架构节点。
理解，scourge保持为Education_ContentCDN。那conditional_curse的回调描述里提到了Malfeas和Demon-Blooded，这些属于业务隐喻，我保持原样不展开为架构节点，对吗？
正确，那些是业务规则描述。不过conditional_curse里提到的"Calibration"需要理解为定时任务调度机制，对应我们的系统就是定期清理过期标记。
我注意到malediction的描述里提到了Education_ClassroomService，但图上没有直接连接。这是通过回调机制间接引用吗？
是的，ClassroomService是malediction可能调用的下游服务之一，但不是固定拓扑。我们保持这种松耦合，通过配置化Charm选择来实现。scourge里也有类似的ContentCDN和StudentDB回调引用。
已确认所有回调引用保持为描述性内容。现在检查连接完整性：tiny_damnation→imprecation→malediction→scourge，以及imprecation→conditional_curse。共5个主节点，1个子图。
conditional_curse的触发条件描述里提到"positive intent"和"negative intent"两种路径，这在架构上需要区分吗？还是统一走imprecation触发？
当前设计是统一由imprecation触发，内部通过Compassion/Valor参数区分。如果需要显式拆分，我可以添加从Benefaction到conditional_curse的另一条路径。但当前代码里没有Benefaction节点。
保持现状，Benefaction在当前版本未实现。conditional_curse的Prerequisite Charms里提到它，但图上只保留实际存在的imprecation连接。这是有意的设计裁剪。
scourge的描述里有个笔误——"heinous"写成了"he inous"，还有"tor"应该是"for"。这些需要我在回调文本中修正吗？
请修正这些明显的拼写错误。另外scourge里的"l permanent Willpower"应该是"1 permanent Willpower"，数字1被误写为小写L了。

已修正所有拼写错误。最终确认：tiny_damnation[Education_ContentCDN]、imprecation[Education_ContentCDN]、malediction[Education_StudentDB]、scourge[Education_ContentCDN]，以及子图Players Guide中的conditional_curse[Education_StudentDB]。
完美。这个架构体现了分层诅咒——我是说分层缓存的设计：从CDN入口的轻量处理，到学生数据库的深度校验，最终回写CDN，并通过conditional_curse实现延迟触发补偿。准备输出最终图表。
最终图表已生成，包含完整回调描述和跨子图连接。所有节点标签、类型标注和依赖关系与需求一致，主题设置为dark模式。
